title: Ericoides umbelliflorum
taxonomy: Ericoides umbelliflorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a type of vine or climber that is native to parts of Asia. The exact classification of Ericoides umbelliflorum can be broken down further into the genus (Ericoides) and species (umbelliflorum) levels. The genus Ericoides is sometimes considered to be synonymous with the genus Rauvolfia. However, more research is needed to confirm the classification of Ericoides umbelliflorum.

intro: Ericoides umbelliflorum, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antivenom properties. The bioactive constituents of Ericoides umbelliflorum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, and is also used in the treatment of scorpion stings and snake bites in some regions.
